questions.question: What is the main difference between StringBuffer and StringBuilder?
expectedPoints: StringBuffer is slower due to synchronization
StringBuilder is faster in single-threaded environments

questions.question: Explain with examples three important methods of StringBuffer class.
expectedPoints: append() - adds data to the end
insert() - inserts data at specified position
delete() - removes characters from specified range

questions.question: When would you prefer StringBuilder over StringBuffer? Justify your answer.
expectedPoints: When thread safety is not required
StringBuilder has lower overhead due to lack of synchronization

questions.question: Compare and contrast String, StringBuffer and StringBuilder classes.
expectedPoints: String is immutable, others are mutable
StringBuffer is thread-safe, StringBuilder is not

questions.question: Explain the capacity management in StringBuffer with examples.
expectedPoints: Difference between capacity() and length() methods
Benefits of capacity management for performance
